original_sentence: Many NLP applications require detecting relations between word meanings beyond synonymy and paraphrasing.
new_sentence: Beyond synonymy and paraphrasing, numerous NLP applications need to identify relationships between word meanings.

original_sentence: While prior work has defined lexical entailment as a relation between word types (Turney and Mohammad, 2013), we argue entailment relations are better defined when illustrating word meaning with an example in context.
new_sentence: Lexical entailment has been conceptualized in earlier work as a word type relation (Turney and Mohammad, 2013), but we propose that entailment relations receive more effective definition when word meaning is demonstrated through contextual examples.

original_sentence: The championship game was played in NYC.
new_sentence: In NYC, the championship game took place.

original_sentence: The hunters were interested in the big game.
new_sentence: The big game captured the hunters' interest.

original_sentence: We would like to directly assess the sensitivity of our models to contexts that signal different word senses, as well as quantify the extent to which our models detect asymmetric entailment relations rather than semantic similarity.
new_sentence: We aim to conduct a direct evaluation of our models' responsiveness to contexts indicating various word senses, and to measure the degree to which they recognize asymmetric entailment relations rather than semantic similarity.

original_sentence: In this section, we aim to further test the assumptions underlying our proposed context representations.
new_sentence: Further examination of the assumptions supporting our proposed context representations constitutes the primary objective of this section.
